{
  "purpose": "This code extends the jQuery Validation plugin to display validation error messages as Bootstrap tooltips, supporting multiple Bootstrap versions and configuring tooltips based on data attributes and defaults.",
  "sources": "Reads data attributes (e.g., data-html, data-placement, data-trigger, data-delay, data-container) and element attributes (like 'title') from DOM elements, as well as the error list generated by the validation plugin.",
  "sinks": "Uses Bootstrap tooltip API to display error messages as tooltips on form elements, and modifies element classes to reflect validation state.",
  "flows": "Error list triggers tooltip display with messages; data attributes and element attributes provide configuration; valid elements have tooltips disposed and classes updated; tooltip options are assembled from defaults and data attributes.",
  "anomalies": "No suspicious code, obfuscation, or external network activity; standard use of jQuery and Bootstrap APIs; no hardcoded secrets or backdoors.",
  "analysis": "The code dynamically detects Bootstrap version and applies appropriate tooltip methods. It reads configuration from data attributes, which is common but could be manipulated if untrusted. No malicious behavior, code injection, or data exfiltration is present. The logic for showing and hiding tooltips aligns with standard validation UI patterns. The code is clear, well-structured, and uses standard practices. The main potential concern is reliance on untrusted data attributes for tooltip configuration, but this is typical in UI code and not inherently malicious.",
  "conclusion": "The code is a benign, standard extension for displaying validation errors via Bootstrap tooltips. It contains no malware, obfuscation, or malicious intent. The low security risk score is justified, primarily due to data attribute usage which is common in client-side UI scripts. Overall, the code is safe and appropriate for production use.",
